name: Donia Skima
role: ERP / Software Developer specializing in Acumatica

experience.title: Experience
experience.description: ERP and software engineer focused on Acumatica: customizations, integrations, and business workflows. I also ship web and mobile products with React/NestJS/Spring Boot, wire APIs, and keep deployments stable while teams iterate.

hard-skills.title: hard-skills
hard-skills.description:
ERP & Business Systems:
Acumatica customization, screens, GI/BQL, imports, business logic

Frontend:
React, TypeScript, Next.js

Backend:
Node.js, NestJS, Spring Boot

Data & Integration:
REST APIs, OData, SQL, MongoDB, RabbitMQ

DevOps & Delivery:
Docker, CI/CD

soft-skills.title: soft-skills
soft-skills.description:
Clear communicator, collaborative by default, and calm when things get messy.

I like clear goals, fast feedback, practical problem-solving, and keeping people aligned without unnecessary noise.

bio.title: bio
bio.description:
Donia Skima — most of what I build lives where Acumatica ERP, integrations, and web applications meet real business problems: quiet on a slide deck, obvious when something stops breaking every Monday.

Customizations, automation flows, and connected systems are my usual lane—ERP and backend first, with a modern UI when the plan needs it. I would not call myself a frontend fan; I still want the screen to feel calm and the logic behind it to stay maintainable.

Typical threads look like this:

  • Acumatica / Haufe X360 customization
  • API and third-party integrations
  • React / TypeScript development
  • Business process automation

I enjoy turning knotty workflows into clean solutions—practical, scalable, and boring-in-production in the right way.

And from time to time, yes, I still vibe-code a frontend when the feature deserves a polished face.

Guilty pleasure: I can't fall asleep without murder cases, psychopath documentaries, or serial-killer series murmuring in the background. Yes, I know, I know—embarrassing. For the record: I'm not a psychopath. I swear.

interests.title: interests
interests.description: Painkiller features, useful customizations, digging deeper into ERP logic, and figuring out why a process is broken before everyone else decides it is “just how the system works.”

education.title: education
education.description: Computer Science background in Monastir, with studies at ISIMM — conveniently located right in front of the beach, which was both inspiring and slightly unfair during exam season.

favorite-games.title: favorite-games
favorite-games.description: I tried Valorant with friends and discovered that precise shooting is not really my calling. Give me an impostor game, hidden roles, and suspicious behavior instead.

1.description: The project, for an industrial climbers company located in Germany, is an extensive endeavor focused on creating a robust and scalable platform by integrating both existing and newly developed microservices.

2.title: Incedo Lead Generator
2.description: I contributed to the project The Incedo Lead Generator, an innovative platform that enables precise targeting of your clearly defined target group. Whether in recruiting to find the right candidates or in sales to generate promising leads, through intelligent automation.

5.title: Plot
5.description: I contributed to the Plot project as a frontend developer freelancer. Plot is a platform that makes social videos smarter. I worked on various frontend tasks, delivering key features and improvements.

6.title: react-crafitify
6.description: React-Crafitify is a versatile React component library designed to enhance the user interface of your web applications. With a focus on aesthetics and usability, React-Crafitify provides a collection of customizable UI components that can seamlessly integrate into your projects.

7.title: cmdbook-cli
7.description: Just-for-fun CLI project I am developing to explore terminal UX and tiny utilities.

8.title: ask-cli
8.description: A playful CLI project I am developing just for fun to experiment with prompts and helpers.
